steps.title: Follow the underestimate logic
steps.content: The proposed explanation is conditional: if clustered camera trapping and GPS collar tracking tend to underestimate cougar density, then a study using those methods should be the one with a notably low density estimate. The data must connect both the method and the reported maximum density.

steps.title: Match method to low result
steps.content: Murphy and colleagues used the named methods and reported a maximum density of only 1.65 individuals per 100 square kilometers, lower than several other studies in the table. Choice A relies on a comparison involving biopsy darting, which is not one of the suspected underestimating methods. Choice C compares study-area size rather than density, and Choice D gives a high density rather than an underestimated one. The answer is B.
